changes_made: Renamed 'k8s_audit_policy_captures_source' to 'k8s_audit_policy_source_capture_enabled' for consistency and clarity
review_notes: The control AU-3-d is applicable to Kubernetes as it relates to audit logging capabilities of the API server. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions but needed slight adjustment for clarity and consistency. The function 'k8s_audit_policy_source_capture_enabled' better describes the check for capturing the source of events in audit logs.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the specific check being performed, such as enabling source capture in audit policies.
title: Content Of Audit Records - Source of the event;
step1_reasoning: The control AU-3-d focuses on ensuring that audit records capture the source of events. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to the audit logging capabilities of the API server. The function 'k8s_audit_logging_enabled' ensures that audit logging is active. 'k8s_audit_policy_captures_source' would verify that the audit policy is configured to capture the source of events, such as user identity and request origin. 'k8s_audit_log_retention_configured' ensures that logs are retained for a sufficient period to allow for auditing and forensic analysis.
